issues.criterion: The whole point of C12 is remediating NDEBT-009 (dormant fixture-CI: a validator regression that made a check vacuously pass would not be caught because no CI job exercises the fixtures). A check whose OWN meta-risk -- silently passing vacuously if it stops inspecting anything -- is unpinned by any acceptance test would recreate the exact debt class it exists to remediate.
issues.evidence: Built a scratch prototype implementing check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures exactly per design_notes.mechanism_chosen (globs tools/fixtures/{ecosystem_baseline,preflight_verdict,engineering_finding}_*.json, python3+jsonschema per family, positive-must-validate/negative-must-reject), wired into the full sweep only, in a disposable mktemp clone of real HEAD d99d57a (never touching the real repo tree; confirmed via git status --porcelain before/after). Baseline: full sweep = 'SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed', exit 0 -- all 15 of the contract's own literal verification commands were extracted from .agent/contracts/042.json and re-executed verbatim against this prototype; all 15 print PASS. Vacuity probe: in a second disposable clone, moved ONLY the three families' fixture files out of tools/fixtures/ (a clean simulation of the exact NDEBT-009 dormancy scenario -- a directory rename/relocation -- leaving every other fixture and doc untouched). Re-ran the full sweep: '[C12] PASS ecosystem-fixtures' with zero files matched by the glob, and the sweep still prints 'SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed', exit 0. Scanned all 15 verification entries in .agent/contracts/042.json: none asserts a minimum fixture count, asserts the glob matched at least one file per family, or otherwise pins a non-zero-files-validated invariant for C12.
issues.required_fix: Add a verification entry (mirroring C11's own existence-guard design intentionally does NOT apply here -- C11 is enforce-if-present/skip-if-absent by design for consumer-safety, but C12's three families are framework-owned fixtures that are ALWAYS expected to exist in this repository, so zero-match must FAIL, not skip) that proves C12 FAILS loudly when its glob matches zero files for any of the three families -- e.g. in a disposable clone, `mv tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_*.json <elsewhere>` then assert the full sweep's C12 line is FAIL (not PASS) and the overall sweep is non-zero exit. This is the single highest-priority fix: it is the dormancy-regression guard NDEBT-009 itself demands, and it is not currently acceptance-tested at all.

issues.criterion: NDEBT-014 CLASS-4 REGRESSION GUARD: the negative-marker regex is immune to the 'invalid' contains 'valid' trap, including on an adversarial hypothetical filename.
issues.evidence: Entry 9's command is a fully standalone python3 -c snippet that RE-DECLARES its own literal copy of `NEGATIVE_TOKEN = re.compile(r'(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)')` and tests it against six hardcoded strings. It never reads, greps, imports, or otherwise sources the ACTUAL regex that will ship inside tools/validate.sh's check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures function. This is precisely the class of defect this repository's own history flags as blocking: contract 041's round-0 HIGH finding (closed at rev1, see .agent/qa/041-contract-review-rev1.json) required truth-checking claims against the REAL artifact rather than a schema-shaped-but-untruthful proxy; here the proxy is a hand-duplicated regex literal, not the shipped code. Confirmed by direct comparison: my own prototype's shipped regex (embedded in check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures, tools/validate.sh line ~1375) is byte-identical to entry 9's copy ONLY because I copy-pasted it deliberately when building the prototype -- nothing in the contract's verification suite would catch a generator who ships a DIFFERENT regex (e.g. adds re.IGNORECASE, mistypes a character class, or replaces it with a naive `'valid' in name` substring check) while entry 9 continues to pass against its own untouched, hardcoded copy. Entries 5-7 (the load-bearing proofs) DO exercise the real shipped code, but only against the three EXISTING, already-correctly-named fixtures -- none of them exercises a hypothetical/adversarial filename (e.g. the 'ecosystem_baseline_revalidated.json' case entry 9 claims to cover) against the real implementation.
issues.required_fix: Rewrite entry 9 so it truth-checks the SHIPPED artifact, not a duplicate: either (a) extract the actual NEGATIVE_TOKEN pattern string from tools/validate.sh via a targeted grep/python read before testing it against the six (or more) cases, so a divergent shipped regex fails the test; or (b) drop the six abstract filename cases and instead materialize the adversarial hypothetical filename(s) as real, disposable fixture files inside a mktemp clone (mirroring entries 5-7's own proven pattern) and run the actual `bash tools/validate.sh` sweep against them, asserting the correct PASS/FAIL direction end-to-end through the real code path.

issues.criterion: Load-bearing bidirectionality per family: prove that BOTH a negative-fixture-made-valid AND a positive-fixture-broken flip C12 to FAIL, for EACH of the three families.
issues.evidence: Entries 5-7 test exactly one direction per family (baseline: negative-made-valid; finding: positive-broken; preflight_verdict: negative-made-valid) -- three of the six possible (family x direction) combinations. I independently constructed and ran the three UNTESTED combinations against my prototype in fresh disposable clones: (a) ecosystem_baseline_valid.json with 'framework_references' stripped -> '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures ... expected to validate ... but was rejected', sweep exit 1; (b) engineering_finding_neg_missing_owner.json patched to add a fabricated 'owner' field (making it schema-valid) -> '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures ... expected to be REJECTED ... but validated', sweep exit 1; (c) preflight_verdict_pass_with_exceptions.json with 'operator_approval' stripped -> '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures ... expected to validate ... but was rejected', sweep exit 1. All six combinations correctly flip C12 to FAIL under my family-generic prototype (a single loop over a FAMILIES dict, identical logic per family, matching design_notes' description). Because the design commits to one uniform code path across all three families with no per-family branching, the risk of an asymmetric bug surviving the 3-combination sample is low but not structurally guaranteed until the real implementation lands -- a generator that special-cases one family (e.g. different required-field handling) could still pass entries 5-7 while shipping a broken sibling direction.
issues.required_fix: Given this contract's own framing that C12 modifies the validator gate itself (elevated meta-risk), add the three missing (family x direction) combinations as additional verification entries, mirroring entries 5-7's exact pattern -- this is a low-cost, high-assurance addition (three more mktemp-clone one-liners) that closes the coverage gap outright rather than relying on an inferred uniformity assumption about not-yet-written code.

missing_acceptance_coverage: Zero-match / dormancy-regression guard for check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures (see HIGH issue above) -- no entry proves C12 fails loudly when its glob matches no files.

Family x direction combinations: ecosystem_baseline positive-broken, engineering_finding negative-made-valid, preflight_verdict positive-broken (see MEDIUM issue above) -- only 3 of 6 combinations are covered by entries 5-7.
unsupported_claims: design_notes.negative_marker_design_and_ndebt_014's claim that 'the regex is immune to this trap ... proven by verification entry 9' is not actually proven against the shipped artifact -- entry 9 tests a hand-duplicated standalone copy of the regex, not the code that will ship inside tools/validate.sh (see HIGH issue above, verification_index 9).
observations: CLASSIFICATION-RULE RESIDUAL RISK FOR FUTURE FIXTURES (non-blocking for this contract's own scope, since 042 creates no new fixture): directly executed NEGATIVE_TOKEN against additional adversarial filenames not in entry 9's case list. Confirmed dodges: 'ecosystem_baseline_negative_x.json' (full word 'negative', not the token 'neg' immediately delimited) does NOT match -> misclassified positive; 'ecosystem_baseline_NEG_case.json' / '..._Neg_case.json' (uppercase/mixed-case) do NOT match (the regex carries no re.IGNORECASE) -> misclassified positive. Confirmed false-trigger: a hypothetical genuinely-POSITIVE fixture descriptively named '..._invalid_handling.json' (documenting invalid-input HANDLING, not itself invalid) WOULD match -> misclassified negative, blocking CI for a legitimate future fixture. None of these affect the 15 fixtures shipped today (all 15 verified correctly classified by the prototype: full sweep = 12 passed, 0 failed). The narrow but real compound risk: a future negative fixture mislabeled with one of the two 'positive' dodges above that ALSO happens to accidentally schema-validate would silently escape C12 (misclassified positive, and happens to pass) -- a human-naming-error path to the same vacuous-pass failure mode as the HIGH zero-match finding above, just triggered by a typo/case slip instead of a directory rename. Recommend logging a candidate NDEBT entry for this (paralleling how design_notes.known_preexisting_gap_not_fixed_here already flags the --target routing collision as a candidate NDEBT entry rather than fixing it in-contract), or extending entry 9's adversarial case list to include case-insensitive and full-word forms in a future rev.

SUMMARY-MIGRATION SWEEP (probe 4, clean result): grepped docs/guide/index.html and methodology/ directly for hardcoded '11 passed' / 'C1-C11' / '11 checks' strings -- zero hits in either. The one LIVE doc that does carry these strings today (tools/README.md, lines 11/63/68) is exactly the file this contract's own scope already edits. CHANGELOG.md, docs/planning/DEBT.md, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, and .agent/product_spec_004.md also carry historical '11 passed'/'C1-C11' text but are correctly out of this contract's non_goals (CHANGELOG.md deferred to feature 045 per established precedent; the planning docs and the frozen phase-004 spec are historical/out-of-scope). No missed live-doc drift found.

--target FENCING RE-CONFIRMED (probe 5a): `bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json` and `--target tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_pass.json` were both re-run against the prototype -- C12 never appears in --target output (grep -F '[C12]' finds nothing), confirmed identically absent for both a non-fixture and a fixture target.

PRE-EXISTING --target ROUTING COLLISION CONFIRMED FENCED, NOT WORSENED (probe 5b): `bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_pass.json` produces the byte-identical failure ('[C11] FAIL dogfood --target routed: qa_verdict ... schema violation (qa_verdict): 'PASS' is not one of [\'pass\', \'fail\']') on both the real, unmodified repository HEAD and the C12-equipped prototype clone. The pre-existing bug is neither fixed nor worsened by this contract, matching design_notes.known_preexisting_gap_not_fixed_here's claim exactly.

--payload NO-OP RE-CONFIRMED (probe 5c): `bash tools/validate.sh --payload` output contains zero mentions of 'C12' anywhere in its text (not merely an absent PASS/FAIL line), and its SUMMARY line is byte-identical to the pre-042 baseline ('SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed').

NDEBT-014 BRACKET-BUG SWEEP (probe 5d): extracted all 15 verification 'command' strings verbatim from .agent/contracts/042.json via json.load (avoiding any transcription risk) and executed each literally, unmodified, against the prototype clone. All 15 print PASS with exit 0; no quoting, heredoc, or bracket defect found in any of the 15 as currently written. Post-run `git status --porcelain` in the command-execution clone showed no residue from entries 5-7's internal self-cleaning mktemp sub-clones.

All probe work was performed exclusively inside disposable `mktemp -d`-rooted clones under the evaluator's scratchpad; `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` on the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was checked before and after this entire review and shows only the two pre-existing tolerated untracked entries (.agent/contracts/042.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) throughout -- no real repository path was created, edited, or deleted by this review.

final_verdict.summary: NOT approved at revision 0. Two HIGH-severity contract-testability defects found, both directly on-point for this feature's own stated elevated meta-risk (it modifies the validator gate itself): (1) C12's zero-match/dormancy-regression failure mode is completely unpinned by any of the 15 verification entries -- empirically confirmed a scratch prototype built exactly to design_notes.mechanism_chosen PASSES VACUOUSLY (clean 'SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed', exit 0) when the three families' fixtures are moved out of tools/fixtures/, which is precisely the NDEBT-009 dormancy scenario this feature exists to remediate; nothing in the contract would catch this regression landing later. (2) Verification entry 9 (the NDEBT-014 class-4 regression guard) tests a hand-duplicated standalone copy of the negative-marker regex, not the actual code that will ship inside tools/validate.sh -- the same class of defect this repository's own history (041's content-honesty lesson, .agent/qa/041-contract-review-rev1.json) treats as blocking: a test that truth-checks a proxy artifact rather than the real shipped one gives false assurance. A third, MEDIUM finding recommends extending entries 5-7's load-bearing proof to all six (family x direction) combinations rather than three, given the elevated meta-risk framing, though my own independent reconstruction of all six combinations against a family-generic prototype suggests this risk is lower than the two HIGH findings. All other probed areas were clean: the --target/--payload fencing, the pre-existing routing-collision non-regression, the SUMMARY-migration doc sweep, and a literal re-execution of all 15 verification commands (NDEBT-014 bracket-bug sweep) found no additional defects. A residual, non-blocking classification-regex risk for FUTURE (not-yet-created) fixture names is logged as an observation, recommended for a candidate NDEBT entry rather than blocking this contract. required_state: generator_rework -- revise the contract to add a zero-match dormancy guard and repair entry 9 to test the real shipped regex (not a duplicate), then resubmit for a rev1 review.
